changes.description: PATH ERROR IN AUTHORITATIVE INPUTS — ADVISORY. The prompt's 'Authoritative inputs' and 'Inspect all biomarker identity and canonicalisation files' section lists 'backend/config/biomarker_alias_registry.yaml', 'backend/config/biomarker_catalogue.yaml', and 'backend/config/derived_biomarkers.yaml'. None of these files exist at backend/config/. The actual alias registry is at backend/ssot/biomarker_alias_registry.yaml (confirmed via Grep, 199+ lines). backend/config/ contains only Python configuration files (__init__.py, ai.py, settings.py, flags.py, database.py, env.py). No biomarker_catalogue.yaml or derived_biomarkers.yaml exist anywhere in the repo.
changes.citation: CONFIRMED via Glob: backend/config/ contains __init__.py, ai.py, settings.py, flags.py, database.py, env.py only. CONFIRMED via Grep pattern 'dhea|DHEA' on *.yaml: backend/ssot/biomarker_alias_registry.yaml lines 178-194 contain dhea and dhea_s entries.
changes.required_action: Cursor: use backend/ssot/biomarker_alias_registry.yaml as the authoritative alias registry. Document the path correction in the Phase 10 audit paper. Do not attempt to create or populate backend/config/biomarker_alias_registry.yaml.

changes.description: PATH ERROR — PARSING AND NORMALISATION DIRECTORIES DO NOT EXIST. The prompt lists 'backend/core/parsing/**' and 'backend/core/normalisation/**' as inspection targets. Neither directory exists. The actual canonicalisation and normalisation implementation lives in backend/core/canonical/ (alias_registry_service.py, normalize.py, alias_registry.py, resolver.py).
changes.citation: CONFIRMED via Glob: backend/core/parsing/ — no files found. backend/core/normalisation/ — no files found. backend/core/canonical/ contains alias_registry_service.py, normalize.py, alias_registry.py, resolver.py.
changes.required_action: Cursor: the canonicalisation implementation to extend is backend/core/canonical/alias_registry_service.py (AliasRegistryService.resolve()) and backend/core/canonical/normalize.py (BiomarkerNormalizer). Do not create parsing/ or normalisation/ directories.

changes.description: ALIAS REGISTRY CONFIRMED PRESENT WITH DHEA IDENTITY MAPPED TO UNSULFATED DHEA. backend/ssot/biomarker_alias_registry.yaml lines 178-184: dhea entry maps aliases ['DHEA', 'DHEA (Venous)', 'DHEA - venous', 'Dehydroepiandrosterone'] to canonical_id: dhea. Lines 185-194: dhea_s entry maps aliases ['DHEA-S', 'DHEA-S (Venous)', 'DHEA-S - venous', 'DHEAS', 'Dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate', 'dehydroepiandrosterone_sulfate', 'dheas'] to canonical_id: dhea_s. DHEA and DHEA-S are already separate canonical identities. The problem is that 'DHEA (Venous)' is currently hardcoded to resolve to dhea, not dhea_s.
changes.citation: CONFIRMED via Grep on backend/ssot/biomarker_alias_registry.yaml: lines 178-194 (dhea block and dhea_s block both present and separated).
changes.required_action: Cursor: Phase 3 must NOT blindly remap 'DHEA (Venous)' in the alias registry from dhea to dhea_s without a unit/range gate. The unit-aware canonicalisation model must be implemented in code (AliasRegistryService or a new wrapper) before any alias registry change is made. A label-only remap is explicitly prohibited by the sprint's non-negotiable constraints.

changes.description: SSOT BIOMARKERS.YAML — DHEA AND DHEA-S CONFIRMED SEPARATE WITH CORRECT UNITS. backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml lines 1397-1416: dhea entry with unit 'umol/L', no aliases listed, category Adrenal. Lines 1417-1437: dhea_s entry with unit 'umol/L', aliases [dheas, dhea_sulfate, dehydroepiandrosterone_sulfate], description explicitly states 'distinct from unconjugated serum DHEA'. The two canonical biomarkers are correctly separated at SSOT level. No SSOT redesign is required by this sprint.
changes.required_action: Cursor: do not modify backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml unless explicitly justified by Phase 1 identity audit. The SSOT structure is correct.

changes.description: AB FULL PANEL SAMPLE EVIDENCE CONFIRMED. backend/tests/fixtures/panels/ab_full_panel_with_ranges.json line 173: 'dhea' key, value 5.12, unit 'umol/L', reference_range { min: 0.94, max: 15.44, unit: 'umol/L', source: 'lab' }. This range (0.94-15.44 µmol/L) is consistent with DHEA-S reporting conventions, not unsulfated DHEA reporting conventions. The fixture already holds the parsed canonical key 'dhea' — the raw label 'DHEA (Venous)' is not preserved in the fixture. Parsing notes (ab_full_panel_parsing_notes.md line 13) confirm 'dhea' is in the parsed biomarker list.
changes.required_action: Cursor: Phase 1 identity evidence is SUFFICIENT from the fixture — unit umol/L and reference range 0.94-15.44 µmol/L strongly support DHEA-S identity. Note that the fixture does NOT preserve the original raw label 'DHEA (Venous)'. Phase 8 canonicalisation tests must use the raw label form (e.g. 'DHEA (Venous)') as test input, not the already-resolved canonical key 'dhea'.

changes.description: RESOLUTION CHAIN CONFIRMED — DHEA (VENOUS) RESOLVES TO dhea VIA ALIAS REGISTRY. The AliasRegistryService.resolve() method in backend/core/canonical/alias_registry_service.py processes: (1) strip abnormal markers, (2) normalize key to lowercase/underscores, (3) strip specimen suffix '(venous)'. 'DHEA (Venous)' normalizes to 'dhea_(venous)', then strips specimen suffix to 'dhea'. It ALSO matches directly via alias_registry entry at backend/ssot/biomarker_alias_registry.yaml line 182. Both resolution paths currently resolve 'DHEA (Venous)' to canonical 'dhea', not 'dhea_s'. The specimen-suffix stripping alone would prevent unit-aware resolution — Phase 3 must handle this architectural constraint.
changes.citation: CONFIRMED: backend/core/canonical/alias_registry_service.py lines 332-369 (resolve method), lines 314-329 (_strip_specimen_suffix method); backend/ssot/biomarker_alias_registry.yaml line 182.
changes.required_action: Cursor: Phase 3 implementation must address BOTH resolution paths. The alias registry entry for 'DHEA (Venous)' → dhea (line 182) must be handled; and the specimen-suffix stripping logic must not collapse unit-aware DHEA disambiguation. The safest implementation is a post-resolution unit-aware identity refinement step, not a modification to the core specimen-suffix stripping logic which is used for many other biomarkers.

changes.description: PACKAGE PRIMARY_METRIC USES dhea, NOT dhea_s. pkg_kb47_dhea_high_androgen_excess_context/signal_library.yaml line 9: 'primary_metric: dhea'. pkg_kb47_dhea_low_adrenal_androgen_reduction/signal_library.yaml line 9: 'primary_metric: dhea'. Both packages reference the unsulfated DHEA canonical ID. If Phase 4 renames/reroutes the AB panel DHEA to resolve to dhea_s, these packages must be updated to primary_metric: dhea_s. This is a BEHAVIOUR change in signal evaluation scope — signal_dhea_high would then fire only on dhea_s, not dhea. The medical authority (batch2_thyroid_androgen_context_authority_review_v1.md) explicitly requires this: 'DHEA high should not activate unless the source biomarker is clearly DHEA-S'.
changes.citation: CONFIRMED: knowledge_bus/packages/pkg_kb47_dhea_high_androgen_excess_context/signal_library.yaml lines 8-9; knowledge_bus/packages/pkg_kb47_dhea_low_adrenal_androgen_reduction/signal_library.yaml lines 8-9; knowledge_bus/research/medical_reviews/batch2_thyroid_androgen_context_authority_review_v1.md line 20 (DHEA high table entry).
changes.required_action: Cursor: Phase 4 — if DHEA-S identity is confirmed, update both package signal libraries to primary_metric: dhea_s. This change must be listed explicitly in Phase 11 git diff and included in the Phase 10 audit paper's 'files changed' section.

changes.description: DHEA-S HIGH ACTIVATION — PREGNANCY GATE MISSING FROM EXISTING SIGNAL LIBRARY. pkg_kb47_dhea_high_androgen_excess_context/signal_library.yaml runtime_context_requirements lists 6 context gates (sex, age, symptoms_present, supplements_disclosed, hormone_therapy_status_disclosed, aas_exposure_status_disclosed). Pregnancy status is NOT listed. The medical authority review (batch2_thyroid_androgen_context_authority_review_v1.md) requires pregnancy as an EXCLUSION_CONDITION for DHEA high activation. Phase 5 activation gate assessment must flag this missing gate. STOP condition 9 applies if pregnancy context cannot suppress where required.
changes.citation: CONFIRMED: knowledge_bus/packages/pkg_kb47_dhea_high_androgen_excess_context/signal_library.yaml lines 52-70 (runtime_context_requirements — 6 entries, no pregnancy gate); knowledge_bus/research/medical_reviews/batch2_thyroid_androgen_context_authority_review_v1.md line 213 ('Do not emit if pregnancy is disclosed').
changes.required_action: Cursor: Phase 5 — if assessing DHEA-S high activation, the missing pregnancy exclusion gate must be added to signal_library.yaml runtime_context_requirements before activation is permitted. If the pregnancy context primitive cannot be safely encoded from the current questionnaire schema, the outcome must be RENAME_TO_DHEA_S_HIGH_KEEP_INACTIVE_PENDING_GATES with explicit documentation of the missing gate.

changes.description: MEDICAL AUTHORITY DIRECTION IS DO_NOT_ACTIVATE_YET. batch2_thyroid_androgen_context_authority_review_v1.md final activation matrix (line 1100): 'DHEA high | DO_NOT_ACTIVATE_YET_CONTEXT_MODEL_INSUFFICIENT | Future: DHEA-S explicit + sex/age + testosterone/SHBG + supplement exclusions | Yes before activation | LOW'. This direction was confirmed before this sprint and remains the governing medical authority. DHEA-S high activation would require external clinician sign-off per the medical authority matrix. The sprint's Phase 5 outcome is most likely RENAME_TO_DHEA_S_HIGH_KEEP_INACTIVE_PENDING_GATES given the existing authority direction and missing pregnancy gate.
changes.citation: CONFIRMED: knowledge_bus/research/medical_reviews/batch2_thyroid_androgen_context_authority_review_v1.md lines 1100-1101 (final activation matrix rows for DHEA high and DHEA low).
changes.required_action: Cursor: Phase 5 must not activate DHEA-S high without clinician sign-off and all required gates being present. The expected outcome per medical authority is RENAME_TO_DHEA_S_HIGH_KEEP_INACTIVE_PENDING_GATES. Document this explicitly in governance and carry-forward updates.

changes.description: CARRY-FORWARD CF-BATCH2-010 IS OPEN. docs/sprints/launch_core_carry_forward_register.md row CF-BATCH2-010: 'DHEA high/low, FAI low, free testosterone pct high/low remain inactive. CF-BATCH2-010 Open for remaining 5 androgen packages.' This sprint resolves the DHEA identity ambiguity but is unlikely to close CF-BATCH2-010 fully unless DHEA-S high is activated with all gates satisfied. Phase 7 must update CF-BATCH2-010 status accurately.
changes.required_action: Cursor: Phase 7 — update CF-BATCH2-010 in launch_core_carry_forward_register.md. If DHEA-S high remains inactive, do not mark CF-BATCH2-010 as Resolved. Add a new CF item for the DHEA identity resolution outcome.

All three trigger HIGH classification.

BEHAVIOUR classification is mandatory and correct.

DHEA packages must remain inactive unless Phase 5 explicitly activates DHEA-S high with all required gates confirmed

stop_conditions_assessed.stop_2: DHEA and DHEA-S are conflated but cannot be safely separated — CLEAR: they are already separate canonical identities in SSOT

determinism_risks_surfaced: RISK 1 (HIGH): Specimen-suffix stripping in AliasRegistryService._strip_specimen_suffix() already strips '_(venous)' from 'dhea_(venous)' producing 'dhea'. Any unit-aware resolution must happen AFTER the existing alias resolution, not by modifying the specimen-suffix logic which serves many other biomarkers.

RISK 2 (HIGH): 'DHEA (Venous)' maps to dhea via BOTH the alias registry (explicit entry at line 182) AND the specimen-suffix stripping. Phase 3 must handle both paths. Removing the alias registry entry without implementing code-level unit-aware resolution would still result in 'DHEA (Venous)' resolving to 'dhea' via specimen stripping.

RISK 3 (HIGH): Package primary_metric must change from 'dhea' to 'dhea_s' if identity is confirmed. This is a BEHAVIOUR change in signal evaluation scope — the package will no longer fire on a panel that has only unsulfated DHEA. This must be explicitly documented as an intended behavioural change in the audit paper.

RISK 4 (MEDIUM): The AB full panel fixture already holds 'dhea' as the canonical key. Phase 8 canonicalisation tests must simulate the raw input pipeline (input = 'DHEA (Venous)' + unit + range → assert canonical = 'dhea_s'). The fixture itself cannot be used as a test input for the new unit-aware resolution tests without modification.

RISK 5 (MEDIUM): If the governance model at Phase 2 marks runtime_consumed: true but the runtime code is not yet reading it, the validator Phase 9 will fail. The governance file must be marked runtime_consumed: false until runtime code explicitly reads it.

hardening_notes: Sprint is correctly classified HIGH / BEHAVIOUR / TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH. All five front matter fields are valid. Core identity problem is confirmed and verifiable. Path errors exist in the 'Authoritative inputs' section (backend/config/ paths do not exist; use backend/ssot/biomarker_alias_registry.yaml; use backend/core/canonical/ not backend/core/parsing/ or backend/core/normalisation/). AB full panel DHEA sample evidence confirmed at backend/tests/fixtures/panels/ab_full_panel_with_ranges.json lines 173-182: unit umol/L, range 0.94-15.44, consistent with DHEA-S. Critical implementation constraint: 'DHEA (Venous)' resolves to dhea via BOTH alias registry (line 182) AND specimen-suffix stripping — Phase 3 must address both paths without modifying the specimen-suffix logic. Pregnancy gate is absent from DHEA high signal_library runtime_context_requirements — Phase 5 activation is not safe without adding it. Medical authority direction is DO_NOT_ACTIVATE_YET for both DHEA high and low; expected Phase 5 outcome is RENAME_TO_DHEA_S_HIGH_KEEP_INACTIVE_PENDING_GATES. Package primary_metric changes from dhea to dhea_s are required and must be documented as explicit behavioural changes. GPT architectural review and human approval required before merge per HIGH risk route.
